qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Attacker-controlled timestamp strings trigger ReDoS in convict-format-with-moment
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and mechanism: this finding reaches the optional convict-format-with-moment timestamp coercion path and Moment.js parsing, not Convict core load()/loadFile() overlay recursion or schema-initialization writes to Object.prototype described in the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's prototype-pollution root cause in Convict core.
evidence: The advisory describes two prototype-pollution paths in Convict core: overlay() used by config.load()/config.loadFile() and schema initialization that propagates constructor.prototype keys onto Object.prototype.

The only qualifying finding instead targets packages/convict-format-with-moment/src/main.js:43, where timestamp coercion passes attacker-controlled strings into moment(v).valueOf() and triggers slow parsing.
The finding's demonstrated consequence is a local availability slowdown/time-out in timestamp parsing, not prototype pollution through __proto__ or constructor.prototype keys.
